repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body: ## Problem: Currently, there's no way to distinguish progress messages coming from different sources. Nor can Progress event be easily filtered based on source. ## Solution: Add source field to nvim_echo-opts and filter Progress events based on source on pattern. **Test code** ```lua local progress_group = vim.api.nvim_create_augroup('progress-event', { clear = true }) vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('Progress', { group = progress_group, pattern='find:*', desc = 'Prints progress messages', callback = function(ev) vim.print(ev.data) end, }) vim.api.nvim_echo( { { 'searching' } }, true, { id = nil, kind = 'progress', percent = 10, status = 'running', title = 'terminal(ripgrep)', data = {special = true, offset = 10}, source = 'term' } ) vim.api.nvim_echo( { { 'searching2' } }, true, { id = nil, kind = 'progress', percent = 20, status = 'running', title = 'terminal(ripgrep)', data = {special = true, offset = 10}, source = 'find' } ) ``` **Results** Working towards unblocking #36209 cc @mfussenegger @justinmk

comment_data.body: the title is in `data`, why do we need both in the pattern? is there any precedent for this?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -820,13 +820,16 @@ ModeChanged			After changing the mode.  The pattern is
 Progress						*Progress*
 				After a |progress-message| is created or updated via
 				`nvim_echo`. The pattern is matched against
-				title of the message. The |event-data| contains:
+				`source:title` of the message. The |event-data| contains:

comment_data.body:
It just allows filtering based on both source and title. There are previous examples where such composite attribute is used in pattern, `ModeChanged` comes to mind. 

In this case though it's fine if we just use source as pattern. Title can be manually filtered if user want's to in the callback since it's in the data.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -820,13 +820,16 @@ ModeChanged			After changing the mode.  The pattern is
 Progress						*Progress*
 				After a |progress-message| is created or updated via
 				`nvim_echo`. The pattern is matched against
-				title of the message. The |event-data| contains:
+				`source:title` of the message. The |event-data| contains:

comment_data.body: Does this mean these event patterns don't allow wildcards, e.g. `foo*bar` ?
is it intentional that `force=true` was changed to `false` ?

Wildcards work. I was testing with `source:*` earlier when composite was being used. I believe this condition prevents the match from trying to expand the filename. Including directory path doesn't make sense in this case. Without this condition the pattern basically gets converted to `/home/user/dir/dir/.../{source}` . like calling `expand('%:p')` . I guess when autocmd stuff was added files were only form of pattern later this list was added as part of generalization

`force=true` forces `++nested`. For now I can't think of why we should force it. Creating progress-event within progress-handler doesn't feel like a normal usecase. So I've set it to false. Like setting it to least previlized state. If later it's deemed necessary we can enable it.

Specifically this (`nested = true`) is required for the `LspProgress` snippet to work. At the very least, the documented example should be updated.

Full updated example:
```lua
    aucmd('LspProgress', {
      buffer = bufnr,
      nested = true,
      callback = function(pev)
        local value = pev.data.params.value
        vim.api.nvim_echo({ { value.message or 'done' } }, false, {
          id = pev.data.id,
          kind = 'progress',
          source = 'lsp',
          title = value.title,
          status = value.kind ~= 'end' and 'running' or 'success',
          percent = value.percentage,
        })
      end,
    })
```

comment_regular_data.body: @justinmk, can this PR also set appropriate `source` in `vim.pack` (probably `'vim.pack'`?) or should this be done in a separate "many-small-things-at-once" PR? In any case, I think `vim.pack` should also set this as the first core user of the progress API.

> also set appropriate `source` in `vim.pack` (probably `'vim.pack'`?) or should this be done in a separate "many-small-things-at-once" PR?

Please send a micro-pr for that. I wouldn't consider it a "typo", it's a logical change.

@echasnovski I've set source to `nvim.pack` for `vim.pack` and `nvim.health` for `vim.health`.
Those were the only ones with progress support internally for now right?

> @echasnovski I've set source to `nvim.pack` for `vim.pack` and `nvim.health` for `vim.health`. Those were the only ones with progress support internally for now right?

I **really** think that `nvim.pack` is not the right choice here. The name of the module is `vim.pack` and it is already confusing enough for people (as in "Why is it *vim*.pack and not *nvim*.pack?").

The best source name is arguably `vim.pack`. The alternative is `nvim-pack` (used for filetype, confirmation buffer name, etc.), but `vim.pack` is still better I'd say. And translating `nvim-pack:vim.pack` as `source:title` pair is unnecessarily confusing.

Ideally, the `title` then can be something else more informative (like "update", "download", etc.), but it is used when showing progress messages. So it *has to be* `vim.pack` in this case.

> I really think that nvim.pack is not the right choice here. The name of the module is vim.pack and it is already confusing enough for people (as in "Why is it vim.pack and not nvim.pack?").

I've `nvim.` notation because from what I've been seeing we use `nvim.` for naming namespaces & groups.  I guess vim.pack is still fine. Let's not debate on this here. I'll drop the commit and the sources to existing calls can be added on later pr. Main purpose of this pr was to get the internal stuff working.

>  And translating nvim-pack:vim.pack as source:title pair is unnecessarily confusing.

Already have droped the source:title composite. Now the pattern is matched only on source.
Since this defines the event pattern it should probably be required (non-empty after trimming whitespace) when `kind=progress` ? An empty event pattern is probably not good.

> Since this defines the event pattern it should probably be required (non-empty after trimming whitespace) when `kind=progress` ? An empty event pattern is probably not good.

I haven't seen an issue with that yet. But we can make it mandatory.
Note: even if source is not provided we don't set NULL as event pattern it's empty string. Empty string does seem to get handled in autocmd flow from my testning.

This broke sending terminal progress from `LspProgress` via `nvim_echo` (bisected to 4b643d7068e871e4bee8ab74f3f0a054c242fa33) via the example snippet
```lua
    aucmd('LspProgress', {
      buffer = bufnr,
      callback = function(pev)
        local value = pev.data.params.value
        vim.api.nvim_echo({ { value.message or 'done' } }, false, {
          id = 'lsp',
          kind = 'progress',
          title = value.title,
          status = value.kind ~= 'end' and 'running' or 'success',
          percent = value.percentage,
        })
      end,
    })
```
(which worked before that)

> Working towards unblocking https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/36209

Do we really want to push that one day before the planned release?

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/36209 isnt needed for 0.12 but we do want to nail down the Progress interface for 0.12. 

what broke exactly?

> This broke sending terminal progress from LspProgress via nvim_echo (bisected to https://github.com/neovim/neovim/commit/4b643d7068e871e4bee8ab74f3f0a054c242fa33) via the example snippet

Can you elaborate on what's broken? I've added  the snippet in my lsp-onattach. It seems to be working on master.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f5dac748-269f-4597-96a6-3db29f804d53

> Do we really want to push that one day before the planned release?

Sorry about that I wasn't aware of exact date of release. I was just trying to finalize stuff before 0.12

> Can you elaborate on what's broken? 

The native progress bar is no longer displayed (since it's emitted through a default `Progress` autocommand); your terminal apparently doesn't support them. Try Ghostty.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6032701a-0776-4d30-a20a-808b5d375c62
oh it's because of `force=false`. try specifying `nested=true`

Yes, that's exactly what I wrote in the comment chain above. If it's intended that this is now required, that's OK, but then the `:h LspProgress` snippet needs to be corrected accordingly. I just wasn't sure whether `force=false` is "by design" or should be changed, since your comment above sounded equivocal.

The recording was on ghostty. But it seems the native bar doesn't work because tmux sitting between nvim and ghostty.

I think let's keep force=true. Because we Progress event will likely used in UI layer. It's less explaining why nested is needed if being called from within autocmd. We already have progress listerners in core (progress-status), The native progress-bar. Maybe some ui2 integration in future.

Also I noticed if neovim is closed through :quit the progress bar on ghostty doesn't get removed. That's probably a bug.

> Also I noticed if neovim is closed through :quit the progress bar on ghostty doesn't get removed. That's probably a bug.

That was a long discussion about who is responsible for cleaning up the progress bar, with no clear conclusion. The snippet is intentionally simple at the cost of the possibility of stale bars.

(Nested `Progress` autocommands will become a very important issue once `LspProgress` is deprecated since you'll then necessarily have (LSP) `Progress` triggering (`nvim_echo`) `Progress` and need to be careful about infinite regression. But that's not relevant for 0.12, which needs a quick resolution, either way.)

You could still improve the `:h LspProgress` snippet with the new `source` field (and better `id`):
```lua
        vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspProgress', { buffer = buf, callback = function(ev)
            local value = ev.data.params.value
            vim.api.nvim_echo({ { value.message or 'done' } }, false, {
              id = ev.data.id,
              kind = 'progress',
              source = 'lsp',
              title = value.title,
              status = value.kind ~= 'end' and 'running' or 'success',
              percent = value.percentage,
            })
          end,
        })
```

> That was a long discussion about who is responsible for cleaning up the progress bar, with no clear conclusion. The snippet is intentionally simple at the cost of the possibility of stale bars.

but on Nvim *exit* we should cleanup anything we did, no?

Well, in that case it's what a _user_ did (it sent a start without a stop), so it's not obvious whether that is Nvim's problem or responsibility... (We could send an `end` OSC on exit unconditionally, but that feels excessive to me.)

> Well, in that case it's what a _user_ did (it sent a start without a stop), so it's not obvious whether that is Nvim's problem or responsibility... (We could send an `end` OSC on exit unconditionally, but that feels excessive to me.)

In this case what's emitting the special escape-sequences to the terminal is part of neovims codebase. So I feel like we should clean up if we exit early.

A user can send _arbitrary_ escape sequences to the terminal (using Nvim API) -- I don't think it's reasonable to expect Nvim to clean up after those?

And at least Ghostty cleans up stale progress bars after a few seconds by itself, so I don't see a big issue here.

title: fix(progress): allow source of progress message to be specified
